name: Brooks Kerr
abstract: Chester Monson Brooks Joseph Kerr III (December 26, 1951 – April 28, 2018) was an American jazz pianist. He was perhaps best known for being bandleader of a small group featuring Sonny Greer and Russell Procope and for his knowledge of Duke Ellington's work, which he often performed.

concerts.label: The New York Jazz Repertory Company: Duke Ellington 75th Birthday Salute

works.t: Black, Brown and Beige: Come Sunday
I Got It Bad (and That Ain't Good)

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Brooks Kerr, a young man in New York, asked me to ask you about Shep Allen. ... [INTERVIEWEE] I don't know that, either.

about_quotes.speaker: Theodore Saunders
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I can't think of his name. He has very bad sight, and Russell Procope -- and the three of them --

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmie Crawford
about_quotes.evidence: He was good in Duke Ellington's band. Other than that band, I never heard him in no other band.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: When I heard you with Brooks Kerr at Fortune Garden, you were playing tenor.

about_quotes.speaker: Roy Eldridge
about_quotes.evidence: Something similar to what Brooks Kerr had before he went completely blind.
